players.verdictLine: Technically refined in tight spaces, he operates between the lines with a clarity that most players his age don't develop for years. The ceiling here is genuinely high.

description: Operates in the half-space behind the main striker, receiving between the lines and linking play through combination sequences rather than attacking depth. Needs freedom to roam, dictate tempo, and arrive late into the box.
Best in a 4-2-3-1 or 4-4-2 diamond where the #10 position is granted license to drop and receive. Struggles in pure single-striker high-press systems.

onBall.title: Plays comfortably with back to goal.

offBall.text: Vocal presence and visible leadership on the pitch.

pure.name: Technical ball control under pressure

pure.name: Combination play in tight spaces

pure.name: Finishing technique variety
pure.desc: Capable of placements, volleys, mid-range strikes, and weaker-foot finishes. Technical base translates across leagues.

pure.name: Vision & creative passing
pure.desc: Identifies forward solutions between lines consistently. Not inflated by transition-only environments.

context.name: Mid-range shot threat
context.desc: More effective in leagues that allow space at the edge of the box. Compact blocks reduce shot quality significantly.

context.name: Back-to-goal play vs moderate physicality
context.desc: Touch and control hold up against average defenders. Elite physical pressure narrows the margins considerably.

system.name: Free-roaming second striker role
system.desc: Most effective when granted freedom behind a main striker. Output drops in rigid single-striker or high-press-only systems.

system.name: Ball-dominant orchestration
system.desc: Impact rises when allowed repeated touches to feet. Influence reduces sharply in vertical, low-touch roles.

exposed.name: Lack of first-step explosiveness
exposed.desc: Limited separation over first meters. High-tempo leagues reduce time on ball and expose this gap quickly.

exposed.name: Counter-press intensity
exposed.desc: Energy after loss inconsistent. Risks becoming a luxury profile in pressing-heavy systems.

exposed.name: Physical robustness in duels
exposed.desc: Frame solid but not dominant. Sustained contact reduces effectiveness in physical leagues.

prose: Strong base of transferable technical traits, particularly in tight-space control, combination play, and spatial intelligence. Adaptation is likely across multiple tactical environments where tempo is not purely acceleration-driven.

The 7 reflects genuine confidence in the technical and cognitive base. The ceiling on this score is the physical limitations — specifically the first-step gap and inconsistent pressing. In a system that protects him from those exposures early, he develops quickly. In one that doesn't, the adaptation period gets longer.
Success depends on avoiding systems that magnify first-step limitations or demand constant high-intensity pressing without reward.

leagueScores.name: Premier League
leagueScores.note: Physical demands expose the first-step gap. Needs structural protection — hard to guarantee in England.

leagueScores.name: La Liga
leagueScores.note: Positional structure and controlled tempo suit him well. One of his two best T5 fits right now.

leagueScores.name: Bundesliga
leagueScores.note: Transition speed and pressing intensity expose the acceleration gap. Functional, not optimized.

leagueScores.name: Serie A
leagueScores.note: Compact, intelligence-rewarding systems. Link-up and finishing technique translate cleanly here.

leagueScores.name: Ligue 1
leagueScores.note: Athletic duels and recovery speed matter more. Technical base keeps him competitive but not dominant.

leagueScores.name: Eredivisie
leagueScores.note: High lines create space for late arrivals. Technical freedom suits his tempo-control game.

players.trajectoryNote: The shaded band around the projected trajectory reflects genuine uncertainty — player development is volatile, shaped by environment, playing time, injuries, and coaching. The gap between the technical development potential and physical curve is the key variable: if it narrows between ages 19–22, the overall tier accelerates. If physical maturation stalls, the right system becomes non-negotiable.

verdict.statement: Technically refined shadow striker with elite tight-space control, advanced spatial awareness, and varied finishing technique. Best suited to structured possession systems where intelligence and combination play are valued over raw explosiveness. The physical ceiling is the question — but at this age, with this technical base, that question feels answerable.
travels: Elite first touch and ball control in pressure zones
High-level link-up and combination quality
Strong spatial awareness between the lines
Varied and technically clean finishing profile
Composed in decisive moments

Counter-press intensity inconsistent
Physical duel robustness still developing
Role specificity limits plug-and-play versatility
verdict.potentialProjection: Projects as a high-level UCL starter capable of shaping games through intelligence and technical authority. Ceiling depends on physical maturation — likely to grow with age — and whether he can maintain influence without structural protection.

players.verdictLine: A young hybrid #6 with real defensive instincts and composure beyond his age, already comfortable anchoring midfield phases. His game is built on timing, awareness, and control rather than volume or dominance — which gives him a strong base but leaves clear next steps.

description: A modern pivot capable of anchoring the midfield defensively while also driving forward into more advanced zones. Reads second balls early, screens well in central areas, and provides structure without sacrificing forward momentum entirely.
Best in a double pivot or as a single #6 in a team with clear positional discipline. Needs a progressive partner or licence from the coach to step — otherwise his vertical ceiling goes untapped.

offBall.text: Clear room to grow in decisiveness and forward ambition.

pure.name: Defensive positioning and lane-blocking
pure.desc: Reads the game early and positions centrally to cut passing options. Consistent across game states, not system-dependent.

pure.name: Ball security under pressure
pure.desc: First touch and retention in tight central areas hold up against pressure. Translates across multiple tactical environments.

pure.name: Second-ball anticipation
pure.desc: Reacts early to loose balls and regains possession reliably. Functional across structures, not dependent on a specific system.

pure.name: Defensive duel timing
pure.desc: Steps into challenges with conviction based on anticipation rather than athleticism. Transferable at the core.

context.name: Press intensity and counter-press activity
context.desc: More effective in systems that are well-organised defensively. Isolated pressing demands without structure overexpose his energy management.

context.name: Aerial duels
context.desc: Timing can compensate for physical authority in moderate aerial contests. Elite aerial battles at higher levels reduce effectiveness.

system.name: Double pivot structure
system.desc: Impact is highest with a defined partner who takes the progressive role. Output drops when asked to combine both anchoring and driving functions alone.

system.name: Positional clarity from coach
system.desc: Executes a well-defined role effectively. Growth depends on moving from executing to dictating — requires coaching intent.

exposed.name: Limited vertical ambition on the ball
exposed.desc: Prefers security over progression at this stage. High-tempo leagues demand more line-breaking — this gap is immediately targetable.

exposed.name: Forward overcommitment

exposed.name: Aerial authority in contested duels

devPriorities.title: Develop vertical passing intent.
devPriorities.text: The most important on-ball upgrade needed. Must move from recycler to line-breaker to compete at higher levels.

devPriorities.title: Build aerial authority in contested duels.
devPriorities.text: Timing helps but isn't sufficient — physical assertiveness in the air needs deliberate development.

devPriorities.title: Calibrate press triggers to avoid leaving space.
devPriorities.text: Occasional overcommitment is the main structural risk — learning when not to step is as important as stepping.

devPriorities.title: Grow from executor to dictator.
devPriorities.text: Executes a defined role effectively. The next level requires imposing on games, not just fitting into them.

prose: Strong base of transferable traits that adapt to multiple environments, yet weaknesses can be targeted without proper development. Success depends on avoiding systems that exaggerate aerial progression limitations.

The 6 reflects a player who is ready to step up but hasn't yet demonstrated the vertical threat or aerial assertiveness needed to score higher. The defensive tools translate — the progressive side of his game is still catching up.
In a well-structured double pivot or with a coach who actively coaches verticality into him, that number moves. Right now it's honest.

leagueScores.name: Premier League
leagueScores.note: Physical and tempo demands would likely rush his decision-making and overexpose his limited vertical ambition. He'd survive moments, but development would skew toward containment rather than growth.

leagueScores.name: La Liga
leagueScores.note: Tactical rhythm and emphasis on positional discipline suit his reading of the game. However, the slower pace may not consistently force improvement in his assertiveness or aerial competitiveness.

leagueScores.name: Bundesliga
leagueScores.note: Transition-heavy environments would highlight his defensive instincts and engine, but the speed of play could amplify his hesitancy in progressive actions unless paired with a very structured role.

leagueScores.name: Serie A
leagueScores.note: Strong fit for learning spacing, timing, and defensive nuance as a #6. Would benefit his positional intelligence, though ball progression demands may remain secondary unless deliberately coached.

leagueScores.name: Ligue 1
leagueScores.note: A good balance of physical duels and transitional moments without overwhelming tactical complexity. Offers regular exposure to athletic midfield battles while still allowing room to refine on-ball bravery.

leagueScores.name: Eredivisie
leagueScores.note: Best environment for controlled development. Press resistance and ball security are rewarded, while the league naturally encourages growth in vertical passing and decision-making without punishing early mistakes.

The key variable here is whether his progressive passing and physical assertiveness catch up to his defensive instincts. If they do between ages 20–23, the ceiling accelerates. If they stall, the right system becomes non-negotiable.

His game is built on timing, awareness, and control rather than volume or dominance — which gives him a strong base but leaves clear next steps. The ceiling depends on whether his progressive passing and physical assertiveness develop to match what he already does defensively.

Calm and secure in possession under pressure, especially centrally
Covers ground well and stays connected to the defensive structure
Competitive profile without being chaotic — plays within himself

verdict.potentialProjection: Projects as a reliable top-division midfielder who can anchor or support a double pivot, particularly in leagues that value structure and game intelligence. Ceiling depends on whether his progressive passing and physical assertiveness catch up to his defensive instincts.

players.verdictLine: A 17-year-old captain in the Serbian SuperLiga who ranks 5th in passes to the final third among all U19 midfielders in Europe. The physicality ceiling is real and it needs to be managed. Everything else about this profile is ahead of schedule.

description: Organizes from deep, circulates the ball quickly, and releases with precision rather than carrying or forcing. Prefers short verticals and wall passes to retain possession and progress through lines. Reads defensive shape intelligently — an interceptor, not a tackler.

Best in a double pivot or as a left-sided 8 in a 4-2-3-1, where the right side of the pitch is always available to his stronger foot. Long-term, a commanding left-sided 8 at a possession-oriented club that builds from back to front.

onBall.title: Secure first touch.
onBall.text: Opens body to face forward. Clean reception under pressure — creates time that most players at this age have to fight for.

onBall.title: Quick circulation.
onBall.text: Prefers short verticals and wall passes. Rarely holds — releases early to maintain tempo and keep the ball moving through lines.

onBall.title: Calm under press.
onBall.text: Uses quick feet and rhythm shifts to escape tight zones rather than forcing. Rarely panics. Composure in tight spaces is already a natural trait.

onBall.title: Conservative by design.
onBall.text: Organizer more than line-breaker. Values security of possession and rarely loses it. Risk appetite is measured — this is a cognitive choice, not a technical limitation.

onBall.title: Set-piece quality.
onBall.text: Can take corners and free kicks with finesse delivery. An underrated asset for a player this age — adds value beyond the ninety minutes.

onBall.title: Long diagonals — inconsistent.
onBall.text: Looks for switches when the moment is right, but execution at range remains unreliable. The range is there; the consistency under pressure is still developing.

offBall.title: Intelligent positioning.
offBall.text: Closes passing lanes rather than chasing the ball. Anticipates well, stays connected to defensive shape, and is rarely caught out of position.

offBall.title: Interceptor, not tackler.
offBall.text: Prefers stepping into spaces rather than engaging physically. Works with his brain, not his body — which is the right approach given his frame.

offBall.title: Determined presser.
offBall.text: Focused and covers ground relentlessly in transition. When the trigger is there, the press is committed and directional.

offBall.title: Physically bypassed in direct duels.
offBall.text: Lightweight frame and lack of physical bite mean he gets overrun when opponents choose to go through him rather than around him. This is the ceiling constraint.

offBall.title: Mature temperament.
offBall.text: Concentrated, doesn't hide, always offers himself as an outlet. Captain at 17 — the leadership is already embedded in how he plays.

pure.name: Ball security and press resistance
pure.desc: Secure first touch, quick release, calm under press — already formed at 17 and not a product of the current system. This travels across leagues because it is a technical and cognitive habit, not a comfort-zone output.

pure.name: Passing vision and final-third delivery
pure.desc: 5th in passes to the final third among all U19 midfielders in Europe. The vision to find pockets before they close and the precision to thread passes into them is already elite. This is not amplified by the league — it is the trait that makes him stand out in it.

pure.name: Tempo control and scanning
pure.desc: Adjusts game speed cleanly, reads momentum shifts early, scans regularly during buildup. The habit of pre-receive scanning is already in place — most players don't develop this until their mid-twenties if at all.

pure.name: Leadership and maturity
pure.desc: First-team captain at 17, playing alongside players twice his age. The composure, the communication, the refusal to hide — these are character traits that do not change with a transfer. Already one of the strongest psychological profiles in this report archive for his age group.

context.name: Long-range passing variety
context.desc: The diagonal switches and longer distributions are present but inconsistent. They emerge when the system provides width and time — against tighter, higher-tempo structures, the long pass output falls. A real trait, but not yet reliable under duress.

system.name: Defensive coverage volume
system.desc: Active between boxes, but stamina is used more to maintain structure than to win ball aggressively. The defensive numbers at this level are solid — in a higher-tempo league where the defensive workload multiplies, the physical demands of sustaining that coverage change materially.

exposed.name: Physical duel authority
exposed.desc: The defining ceiling question. Lightweight, lacks bite in direct challenges, gets bypassed physically. At 175cm and 17 years old this is partly a maturation question — but it is also the reason he needs a system built around collective structure rather than individual duel-winning. The right environment manages it. The wrong one exposes it immediately.

exposed.name: Pre-receive decision quality
exposed.desc: Quick reactions in tight areas — but pre-receive choices can telegraph passes at times. The scanning habit is there; the disguise in delivery is still developing. Against higher-quality defensive midfielders, the reads will come faster and the telegraphing will be punished.

exposed.name: Aerial presence
exposed.desc: Aerial impact is minimal for his role — currently not a meaningful part of his game. In a true lone pivot role at a higher level, set pieces and second-ball situations in the middle third will test this. Manageable as a second pivot; limiting as a sole 6.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 1.75m, lightweight. Uses both feet well. Agile with quick feet and good rhythm changes — the physical tools that suit his role are present. The tools that don't — power, aerial reach, duel strength — are the ceiling constraints.

athletic.label: Pace
athletic.value: Fast-paced in circulation and transition pressing. Not a burst sprinter but covers ground relentlessly across a full match.

athletic.label: Strength
athletic.value: Weak. Bypassed in direct challenges. Shielding is improving through body angle use, but physical contact situations are a consistent liability right now.

athletic.label: Durability
athletic.value: 2,200 minutes this season. No injuries. The engine is there — he does not break down and does not fade out of matches.

cognitive.label: Decision Speed
cognitive.value: Quick in tight areas. Retains clarity under pressure. Pre-receive choices still developing — can telegraph at times, but the in-play speed is already a strength.

cognitive.label: Scanning
cognitive.value: Regular and disciplined. Head-up frequency during buildup is the cognitive foundation of everything else he does well. Already a habit, not a coached behaviour.

cognitive.label: Risk Profile
cognitive.value: Conservative — organizer more than line-breaker. This is a deliberate cognitive choice and the right one for his age and role. Risk appetite will expand naturally as physicality and range develop.

cognitive.label: Adaptability
cognitive.value: Mid-match adjustments are visible but not yet reliable. Reads momentum shifts early — the translation of that reading into positional adjustment is the developing edge.

psychological.label: Leadership
psychological.value: First-team captain at 17. Leads players twice his age. This is not a ceremonial captaincy — it reflects how his teammates and coaching staff actually read his presence on the pitch.

psychological.label: Composure
psychological.value: Composed after errors. Decision quality stabilizes quickly. Does not spiral or go into his shell — one of the clearest psychological strengths at this age.

psychological.label: Consistency
psychological.value: 2,200 minutes, 7 goals, 4 assists in what is genuinely his second season as a regular starter. Output is not a spike — it is sustained across a full campaign.

psychological.label: National Team
psychological.value: Serbia youth national teams. The pathway is confirmed and the representation validates the level — this is not a player operating only in a comfortable domestic bubble.

devPriorities.title: Physical development — body strength and duel resistance.
devPriorities.text: The most important development priority and the one with the least coaching leverage. Upper body strength, shielding technique under contact, and ground duel assertiveness need to grow materially. Some of this is time — at 17 his frame is still forming. A targeted physical program over the next two to three years is what converts the ceiling question into an answered one.

devPriorities.title: Disguise in distribution.
devPriorities.text: Telegraphing passes in tight areas will be punished at higher levels. The scanning and vision are there — the next layer is disguising the intention before releasing. This is a technical refinement that can be coached directly and is well within reach.

devPriorities.title: Long-range passing consistency.
devPriorities.text: The diagonal switches are in the repertoire but not yet reliable under pressure. Deliberate repetition on weighted distribution at range — particularly from deep-left to wide-right positions — builds the tool that completes his profile as a left-sided 8.

devPriorities.title: Expand the risk register.
devPriorities.text: The conservative risk profile is correct right now. As physicality develops and the system around him becomes more structured, the next growth layer is learning when to break lines rather than always recycling. The vision is there — the trigger calibration is the development task.

prose: The technical and cognitive base travels. Ball security, passing vision, press resistance, tempo control, and leadership are already formed and already producing at a high level in a competitive domestic league. None of that changes with an address.

The score sits at 7.0 because a transfer is a physical exposure event. At 17 and 175cm, moving to a higher-tempo league before the body can handle what better midfielders will do to him physically is the risk. The right next move is an environment where the collective structure protects him — not one that asks him to win individual duels he currently cannot win.

Think: compact shape, possession-based, technically demanding. La Liga and Eredivisie are the natural fits. A double pivot with a physical partner is the deployment that unlocks everything without exposing the gaps. A sole 6 role in a direct league is the deployment that ends careers before they start.

leagueScores.note: Physicality and transition intensity are too demanding right now. The lightweight frame makes him a liability in the duel-heavy midfield battles the Premier League asks for. Only viable long-term with significant physical growth — not a realistic first step from the SuperLiga at 17.

Excellent fit for his composure, short passing, and positional intelligence. A cognitive and technical league where his weaknesses are less immediately exposed. With the right club structure — possession-based, compact collective — this is the ideal developmental environment.

High tempo and pressing traps could overwhelm him. He reads play well, but the vertical chaos and physical midfield contests of the Bundesliga are not the right developmental environment at this stage. System-dependent — workable with the right protection, difficult without it.

Strong tactical fit. Compact spaces, emphasis on positioning, and structured defensive phases align with his intelligence and calmness. Less duel-heavy than the Premier League or Bundesliga. A club that builds from the back and values a technical second pivot is the match.

Physical chaos and duel intensity expose the lightweight frame. Transitional awareness helps, but Ligue 1's open zones and athletic midfield contests make it a difficult environment before physical development catches up.

Ideal developmental fit. High-possession, technically demanding, lower physical thresholds than most top-five leagues. The perfect environment to refine long-range passing, expand the risk register, and develop physically with competitive minutes. Best first step out of the SuperLiga.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: 2,200 minutes at 17, first-team captain, consistent output across a full season, no injuries. Strong upward trajectory. The physical development question keeps this from a 1 — the pathway is clear, the ceiling is real, the timeline is still open.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Mature beyond years, proven adaptability, team-first leadership. Captain at 17 in a professional first-team. No off-field concerns. Thrives under pressure and self-regulates. The psychological profile here is exceptional — among the best in this report archive for his age.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €2M from an under-scouted market, family-represented, no agent inflating the price. Under the radar relative to the output. This is a classic Balkan undervaluation window — the exposure gap between what he is doing and what the market knows about it is still wide open.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: Clearly has a best system — double pivot, possession-based, compact collective, structured rest defense. Output drops meaningfully outside it. Not unusable in other setups, but the physical limitations mean the wrong system doesn't just reduce performance, it exposes it.

players.trajectoryNote: Long-term left-sided 8 or double-pivot organizer at a top-five league club. The ceiling depends on physical development — the base underneath it is already exceptional.

verdict.statement: A lightweight but intelligent organizing midfielder whose technical security and maturity are well ahead of his age. The physicality ceiling is real and it requires the right system to manage — but his intelligence, composure, ball-retention, and final-pass delivery give him a strong and already-functioning base. The long-term ceiling is a commanding left-sided 8 at a possession-oriented club in one of Europe's top leagues. The conditions for reaching it are clear and available.

Secure on the ball under pressure, strong short passing and circulation quality
Mature spatial awareness and tempo control — already a formed cognitive habit at 17
Exceptional leadership and psychological profile — first-team captain, self-regulating, composed
Solid defensive intelligence — reads spaces and intercepts rather than relying on physicality
targeted: Physically weak in direct duels — lightweight frame gets bypassed; needs system protection
Long-range passing inconsistency — 25th-percentile long pass accuracy is an active gap

verdict.potentialProjection: Long-term left-sided 8 or double-pivot organizer at a top-five league club. Consistent, trustworthy, and already producing at an age where most players at this level are still learning to survive senior football. The ceiling depends on physical development — but the base underneath it is exceptional.

players.verdictLine: A 17-year-old at Red Star Belgrade who leads all 60 Serbian SuperLiga midfielders in non-penalty goals, npxG, assists, and box touches per 90. The range is the defining quality: he builds, creates, and scores from the same position, on either foot, in the same match.

description: Operates between the lines and inside the box with equal comfort. Plays with the press resistance and technical calm of a deep builder, but the goalscoring output of a forward, on either foot. Scans before receiving and acts on what he has already seen.
Best behind a single striker with freedom to arrive late into the box and spray passes from the half-space. The position on the pitch shifts depending on what the team needs; the output does not.

onBall.title: Scans before receiving, decides before contact.
onBall.text: Head-up frequency during buildup is already a formed habit. The technical speed this enables — playing quickly in tight spaces without appearing rushed — is the foundation of everything else.

onBall.title: Passes with intent, not just accuracy.
onBall.text: Distribution rarely circulates for its own sake. Each ball either breaks a line, changes the angle of attack, or releases a runner. 100th-percentile long pass accuracy reflects quality and selectivity in combination.

onBall.title: Secure first touch in tight central areas.
onBall.text: Absorbs contact using balance and body positioning. Rarely loses the ball to a bad touch on the receive — the technique is already reliable, not developing.

onBall.title: Finishes with conviction from range.
onBall.text: 97th-percentile npxG per shot reflects good selection as much as technique. He attempts the strikes worth attempting and converts them at an elite rate.

onBall.title: Two-footed without telegraphing the switch.
onBall.text: Uses both feet in combination play and set-piece delivery. The choice of foot does not signal the direction of the pass, which is what makes the two-footedness genuinely dangerous rather than merely functional.

offBall.title: Finds space between the lines before the ball arrives.
offBall.text: Positions off the shoulder of opposing midfielders to receive on the half-turn. The habit of staying off defensive shoulders is consistent across match states.

offBall.title: Times late arrivals into the box precisely.
offBall.text: 98th-percentile box touches and 18 G/A from 23 starts reflect arrival timing as much as positional sense. Arrives when the ball is ready, not before — which means arriving unmarked more often than expected.

offBall.title: Directs teammates on the pitch.
offBall.text: Vocal in buildup, points to space. A natural leadership pattern at 17, as one of the youngest players in a senior squad.

offBall.title: Reactive in the defensive phase.
offBall.text: Intercepts angles rather than hunting duels. 5th percentile in defensive actions. Not a defensive midfielder, and the energy management is deliberate — he conserves output to be available for the moments that matter.

pure.name: Technical press resistance
pure.desc: First touch, ball security, and composure under pressure are already formed at 17 and not system-dependent. Travels across leagues.

pure.name: Two-foot distribution quality
pure.desc: 100th-percentile long pass accuracy without telegraphing foot choice. A genuine two-footedness that makes every pass an option.

pure.name: Clutch goalscoring
pure.desc: 100th percentile np goals per 90. Decisive in high-stakes matches. Derby goals. The mentality is already there.

pure.name: Pre-reception scanning
pure.desc: Already a cognitive habit at 17. The foundation of his technical speed in tight areas — most players don't develop this until their mid-twenties.

context.name: Progressive passing frequency
context.desc: The quality exists at the 100th percentile; the default habit to attempt it does not yet. A system that rewards the attempt is needed before the habit becomes consistent.

system.name: Defensive contribution
system.desc: 5th percentile in defensive actions. System matching matters — a double-pivot partner who handles defensive duties is a structural requirement, not a luxury.

exposed.name: Duel engagement
exposed.desc: 10th percentile. A physical double-pivot partner is a system requirement. The wrong setup asks him to win midfield battles he currently cannot win consistently.

exposed.name: Physical development against mature opponents
exposed.desc: The technical base is there. The sequencing matters. A developmental environment before top-five league exposure is the responsible route.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 1.76m, still developing. The foundation for physicality is present. Whether he learns to impose it in duels is a habits question as much as a size one.

athletic.label: Acceleration
athletic.value: 97th percentile. Gets to speed quickly over short distances, which makes late box arrivals harder to track than his starting position suggests.

athletic.label: Two-footedness
athletic.value: Technically functional on both feet in combination play, distribution, and finishing. Right is stronger; left-foot actions are genuine options rather than forced alternatives.

athletic.label: Durability
athletic.value: No injury history. 23 starts and nearly 2,000 minutes at 17 in a single senior season. Physical availability at this output volume is itself a data point.

cognitive.label: Pre-reception scanning
cognitive.value: The defining trait. Processes the picture before the ball arrives. Already a habit, not coached behaviour — the foundation of his technical speed in tight areas.

cognitive.label: Decision quality
cognitive.value: High. Rarely attempts the progressive ball when it is not on. The 42nd-percentile progressive pass volume is partly this: he chooses the right pass rather than the ambitious one.

cognitive.label: Risk calibration
cognitive.value: Takes risks in the final third rather than in dangerous defensive areas. 84th-percentile fouls drawn reflects a player who invites contact in positions where winning the foul is a positive outcome.

cognitive.label: Spatial awareness
cognitive.value: Constantly shifts position to maintain passing lanes and stay off defensive shoulders. Consistent across match states.

psychological.label: Leadership
psychological.value: Directs teammates on the pitch at 17. Natural inclination toward organising the play around him. Not a coached habit — it is simply how he engages with the game.

psychological.label: Clutch mentality
psychological.value: Derby goals, decisive contributions in high-stakes matches. The mental profile of a player who wants the ball when it matters is already present and observable.

psychological.label: Composure
psychological.value: Unflappable under press. Rarely rushed, rarely panics with the ball. The psychological stability that lets the technical qualities operate under pressure is already formed.

psychological.label: National Team
psychological.value: Called into the senior Serbia squad at 17, not yet earning minutes. The call-up validates the level at which the club performance is being read by the federation.

devPriorities.title: Build the progressive passing habit, not just the quality.
devPriorities.text: Long pass accuracy at the 100th percentile. The frequency of progressive attempts at the 42nd. The gap between what he is capable of and what he defaults to is the most important developmental question — and it requires a system that rewards the attempt before the habit becomes consistent.

devPriorities.title: Physical duel engagement.
devPriorities.text: Duels won at the 10th percentile. Partly a habits question — he does not currently seek contact — and partly a physical development question. A double-pivot partner is the structural near-term solution.

devPriorities.title: Expand the defensive action floor.
devPriorities.text: He is not going to become a defensive midfielder. But modest improvement in defensive volume would meaningfully widen the range of systems he can operate in. A calibration task, not a profile change.

devPriorities.title: Accumulate experience against physically mature opponents.
devPriorities.text: The technical base is there. The sequencing matters. A developmental environment before top-five league exposure is the responsible route and the one most likely to protect what is already exceptional.

prose: The technical and cognitive qualities that make him exceptional are not system-dependent. Press resistance, two-foot distribution, scanning habits, clutch goalscoring — these will show up in any possession-based environment that gives him the ball in central areas. That is the basis for the score.

The discount is honest rather than cautious. Physical maturity against older, stronger midfielders is still an open question. The progressive passing habit needs a system that rewards it before it becomes consistent. The defensive contribution floor is low enough that system matching matters more for him than for most players at this age.

Think: possession-based, technically demanding, double-pivot structure behind him. La Liga and Eredivisie are the natural fits. A transition-heavy system that asks him to win individual duels acquires the wrong version of this player and may not develop the right one.

leagueScores.note: Physical intensity in central midfield would test him before his other qualities could establish themselves. The 10th-percentile duels profile becomes a weekly problem in a league where midfield battles are consistently physical. Long-term ceiling is very high; the immediate environment is wrong.

The best stylistic fit. Possession systems at technical clubs reward the scanning habit, press resistance, and two-foot distribution. A mid-table Spanish club with a structured possession approach gives him license to develop the progressive passing habit in the right environment.
High-tempo vertical play suits his instincts and scanning speed. Physical demands are higher than La Liga. A technical club with a duel-winning double-pivot partner gets the best version.

Tactical compactness and technical demands suit the profile. Ball security and passing intelligence stand out in possession-heavy Italian setups. The emphasis on positional intelligence rather than raw athleticism plays to his natural strengths.

Athletic and physically direct in midfield, which is the profile most likely to limit his influence before it can develop. His technical qualities would show in possession sequences, but Ligue 1's directness would challenge him in defensive transitions.

The natural development bridge. High possession volume, technical identity, a style that explicitly develops creative midfielders. His scanning habits, press resistance, and goalscoring output would be central to a team's offensive identity.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: No injury history. Consistent starter at Red Star at 17, senior national team call-up, strong upward trajectory. The physical development question is the only thing keeping this from a 1.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Leadership, clutch performance, composure under pressure at 17. Derby goals. Senior national team. Nothing in the profile suggests a psychological question worth flagging.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €3.5M for the 17-year-old who leads all SuperLiga midfielders in four attacking categories simultaneously, with no injury history, called into the senior national squad. This valuation does not survive another full season of this output.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: Press resistance, distribution quality, goalscoring are all system-stable. The slight drop outside a possession-based setup with a physical partner alongside him is the reason this is a 2 rather than a 1.

players.trajectoryNote: A 9 projects as a consistent starter and meaningful contributor at a top-five European club, with Champions League impact potential as a creator and scorer from midfield.

verdict.statement: A 17-year-old advanced playmaker who leads all Serbian SuperLiga midfielders in four attacking output categories simultaneously, with no injury history, a senior national team call-up, and a leadership profile that does not usually arrive at this age. The defensive contribution is minimal and system matching matters because of it. The progressive passing habit is developing rather than settled. Both are honest limitations in a profile that is otherwise among the strongest seen at this age in the Balkan region.
travels: Technical press resistance in tight central areas — a formed habit, not a developing tendency
Two-foot distribution quality, including 100th-percentile long pass accuracy without telegraphing foot choice
Clutch goalscoring — 100th percentile np goals per 90, decisive in high-stakes matches
Pre-reception scanning and spatial positioning — already a cognitive habit at 17
Leadership pattern and competitive composure against senior opposition
targeted: Progressive passing frequency — the quality exists; the default habit to attempt it does not yet
Duel engagement — 10th percentile; a physical double-pivot partner is a system requirement
Defensive contribution floor — 5th percentile in defensive actions limits viable system types
Physical development against mature, stronger midfield opponents at the next level
verdict.potentialProjection: A 9 projects as a consistent starter and meaningful contributor at a top-five European club, with Champions League impact potential as a creator and scorer from midfield. The output is already historic for his age in this league. The cognitive and psychological profile suggests the ceiling is real. The honest deduction from a 10 is the defensive contribution floor and the progressive passing habit still forming — not the talent, which is not in question.

players.verdictLine: A 19-year-old at Partizan Belgrade who already knows where the ball is going before most players around him have figured out where it is. The execution lags the spatial intelligence — sometimes significantly — which is why he is still here and not elsewhere.

description: Operates as a vertical connector and second-line attacker. His natural habitat is the space between the opponent's midfield and defensive lines, arriving late into the box with timing that feels pre-programmed. Uses his 1.88m frame to cover ground, shield possession, and win duels without relying on elegance.

Best deployed in a double pivot alongside a true anchor who covers the space behind him when he surges forward. The engine is always running. The question is whether the system asks him to do anything other than run.

onBall.title: Carries with intent through central zones.
onBall.text: Not a decorative carrier. Drives forward to commit midfielders and force defensive reactions, using momentum and long strides rather than close control or feints.

onBall.title: Vertical-minded distributor.
onBall.text: Always looking to break lines or find runners early. Sees ambitious passes consistently, even when the execution — weight, timing, disguise — lags behind the vision.

onBall.title: Arrives to finish, not to circulate.
onBall.text: When he gets on the ball near the box, the mindset is goal-oriented. The 92nd-percentile non-penalty goals reflect this orientation.

onBall.title: Comfortable receiving under pressure — mostly.
onBall.text: Uses his body well to shield, turn, and play forward. The first touch can occasionally escape him at speed — at higher levels, occasional becomes frequent.

offBall.title: Elite second-line runner.
offBall.text: Times late box arrivals from deep with unusual precision. Attacks central channels and arrives unmarked — a major source of his goal threat. This is the trait most likely to transfer cleanly to a higher level.

offBall.title: Presses aggressively in midfield.
offBall.text: Steps forward to engage ball-carriers, closes space quickly, and uses size and reach to disrupt buildup. The engine never stops. The discipline of when not to press is still developing.

offBall.title: Covers large defensive zones.
offBall.text: Tracks runners diligently, shuttles laterally to plug gaps, and recovers quickly when shape breaks. The work rate is not the issue. The question is whether the covering translates to a league where the ball moves faster than he can recover.

offBall.title: Wins duels through timing and power — sometimes.
offBall.text: 58th-percentile aerial duels won is lower than his frame suggests. The physical platform is there. The consistency of applying it is not.

pure.name: Spatial awareness and second-line timing
pure.desc: Always in the right spot at the right time. A natural understanding of spacing that relieves pressure on teammates and pulls defensive structures out of shape. This travels.

pure.name: Engine and work rate
pure.desc: Covers ground, presses aggressively, does not fade. Maintains pressing and late box entries deep into matches. The engine is not a coached behaviour — it is a trait.

pure.name: Composure and big-game temperament
pure.desc: Exceptional for his age. Rises in big matches. Does not hide after mistakes. European football at Partizan has not exposed a psychological ceiling.

context.name: Passing execution and distribution weight
context.desc: The vision is consistently ahead of the execution. Passing weight inconsistent — sometimes too firm, sometimes too soft. In a faster, more demanding environment, the gap narrows but the margin for error also shrinks.

system.name: Defensive role discipline
system.desc: Attacking impact spikes when he has defensive security behind him. In a lone pivot or double pivot without a true holder, his forward surges can destabilize midfield structure.

exposed.name: Technical execution under pressure
exposed.desc: First touch can escape him at speed. When the game slows and he has to create rather than react, he looks clumsier than his frame suggests. The gap between what he sees and executes is the central question of his career.

exposed.name: Aerial duel consistency
exposed.desc: 58th percentile with a 1.88m frame. Lower than it should be. The physical platform is there; the consistent application of it is not. Coachable, but not guaranteed to improve.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 1.88m, still developing. The physical platform is already a weapon. Whether he learns to impose it consistently in duels is an application question, not a size question.

athletic.label: Acceleration
athletic.value: Not explosive off the first step. Needs two or three strides to reach full speed. This matters less for his game — he arrives from deep, not from standing starts.

athletic.label: Endurance
athletic.value: Elite engine. Maintains pressing and late box entries deep into matches. Does not fade.

athletic.label: Strength
athletic.value: Powerful in duels when he engages correctly. Uses hands too much — a correctable habit that currently costs him fouls. The raw strength is there. The technique of applying it is not yet refined.

cognitive.label: Spatial awareness
cognitive.value: The defining trait. Always in the right spot at the right time. A natural understanding of spacing that relieves pressure on teammates and pulls defensive structures out of shape. This travels.

cognitive.label: Decision speed
cognitive.value: Quick when reacting to the game, slower when he has to create. The gap between reactive intelligence and creative intelligence is visible and consistent. He is better when the game comes to him.

cognitive.label: Risk calibration
cognitive.value: Attempts ambitious passes and box arrivals with good frequency. The judgment of when to take the risk is mostly correct. The execution when the risk is taken is not yet reliable.

cognitive.label: Press discipline
cognitive.value: Knows when to press, mostly. Still developing the judgment of when to hold shape rather than commit forward. A club that pairs him with a disciplined anchor solves half the problem.

psychological.label: Composure
psychological.value: Exceptional for his age. Rises in big matches. Does not hide after mistakes. The moment does not shrink him. Rare at any age, vanishingly rare at 19.

psychological.label: Big-game temperament
psychological.value: European football at Partizan has not exposed a psychological ceiling. He has handled the pressure of meaningful matches without regression in output or demeanor.

psychological.label: Resilience
psychological.value: Technical errors do not cascade. Recovers quickly and returns to full intensity within the same sequence. This is what makes the technical gap manageable.

psychological.label: Maturity
psychological.value: 2,000+ senior minutes at 19 in a club with genuine expectations. The context is not junior development football. He has performed where it counts.

devPriorities.title: Technical refinement under pressure.
devPriorities.text: The first touch can betray him at speed. The passing weight is inconsistent. When the game slows down and he has to create rather than react, he looks clumsier than his frame suggests. At SuperLiga level, the margin for error is wide. In the Bundesliga, it narrows immediately.

devPriorities.title: Physical application, not just physical presence.
devPriorities.text: He is 1.88m. He should win more than 58% of his aerial duels. The habit of using hands instead of body positioning is correctable. The question is whether he corrects it before higher-level opponents exploit it.

devPriorities.title: Role discipline without an anchor.
devPriorities.text: His attacking impact spikes when he has defensive security behind him. Learning when to hold and when to go — independent of who is next to him — is the cognitive development task that determines how portable his profile actually is.

prose: The pure traits — spatial awareness, second-line timing, engine, composure — travel unconditionally. They are habits, not products of Partizan's system. A club acquiring Ugrešić knows what he does off the ball. The question is what happens when the technical demands of the new environment expose the roughness in his on-ball execution.

The score sits at 8.0 because the physical and cognitive foundation is strong enough that the technical gaps feel like development tasks rather than ceiling constraints. But that is a feeling, not a guarantee. The Bundesliga is the natural fit — vertical, transitional, tolerant of technical roughness if the engine and physicality are there.

The right environment is one that runs forward and asks him to run with it. A double pivot with a disciplined anchor. A high block that compresses space. A system that values arrival timing over possession maintenance. The wrong environment is one that asks him to slow down and play through pressure. He is not that player yet, and he may never be.

leagueScores.note: Athleticism and work rate translate. The tempo and decision punishment expose technical rawness. Viable with the right role and partner behind him, but not the right first move.
Intelligence and movement fit, but the slower rhythm and technical demands highlight first-touch limitations. The riskiest of the top-five leagues for his profile.

Verticality, transition volume, and emphasis on athletic interiors suit his engine, box-crashing, and pressing instincts. Technical refinement can be developed without suppressing impact. The best fit.
Tactical structure and emphasis on timing favor his spatial intelligence and second-line runs. Needs composure growth, but the profile fits well alongside a true holding midfielder.
Athletic and physically direct in midfield, which suits his engine, but the technical variance in Ligue 1 creates environments where his on-ball roughness is both hidden and then suddenly punished.
Space and tempo allow his box entries and vertical game to shine while smoothing technical edges. Strong developmental environment before a top-five move.

Physically demanding enough to test the application of his frame, but technical thresholds are forgiving. Clubs like Club Brugge or Anderlecht deploy box-to-box midfielders in exactly the double-pivot structure that unlocks his profile.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: 2,000+ senior minutes at 19. Clear upward trajectory. Technical gaps are coachable. The physical and cognitive foundation is strong. The only flag is whether the technical refinement happens quickly enough for the next step.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Exceptional composure for his age. Rises in big matches. Does not hide after mistakes. No off-field concerns. This is the strongest part of the profile.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €6.5M for a 19-year-old starter at Partizan with European experience. Contract until 2030 gives the club leverage. The price is fair for the profile and the upside. Not a bargain. Not a reach.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: Better in some setups than others, but his core strengths — spatial awareness, engine, second-line timing — are not system-dependent. The drop-off outside his ideal environment is manageable.

players.trajectoryNote: Regular starter at a top-division Bundesliga club. Raises team floor through energy, structure, and secondary goal threat rather than serving as a tempo-controlling centerpiece.

verdict.statement: A 19-year-old who already knows where the ball is going before most of the players around him have figured out where it is. That is not a skill you coach. That is a way of seeing the game, and Ugrešić has it. The technical execution is behind the spatial imagination — sometimes significantly behind — which is why he is still at Partizan and not elsewhere. The clubs that understand that his value is structural, not stylistic — that he raises the floor of any midfield simply by being in the right places at the right times — will move now.
travels: Spatial awareness — always in the right place at the right time. Not coachable. He has it.
Second-line arrival timing — 98th percentile npxG, 92nd percentile goals, 5th percentile dribbles. He arrives; he does not create.
Engine and work rate — covers ground, presses aggressively, does not fade
Composure and big-game temperament — the moment does not shrink him. Rare at any age, vanishingly rare at 19.
Physical platform — 1.88m, still developing, already a weapon in duels when he engages correctly
targeted: Technical execution under pressure — first touch can escape him, passing weight inconsistent. The gap between what he sees and what he executes is the central question of his career.
Aerial duel consistency — 58th percentile with his frame is lower than it should be. Coachable, but not guaranteed to improve.
Defensive physicality — covers ground but does not always impose himself. The engine is there. The authority is not yet.
Role discipline without an anchor — needs a holder behind him to unlock his attacking instincts safely.
verdict.potentialProjection: Regular starter at a top-division Bundesliga club. Raises team floor through energy, structure, and secondary goal threat rather than serving as a tempo-controlling centerpiece. The ceiling depends on technical refinement. The floor is already high enough that the 7 feels like a conservative rating — but conservative is where this report should land, because the gap between the SuperLiga and top European leagues is meaningful, and no one should pretend otherwise.

players.verdictLine: Dominant in the air and reliable in duels, Güreler was one of the most complete CB profiles in the Süper Lig last season — technically progressive and physically imposing at just 19.
An aggressive and technically capable fullback who combines defensive solidity with genuine attacking output.

A 17-year-old who scored seven goals in 18 SuperLiga appearances through movement and timing alone, without the technical elaboration you would expect the numbers to require. Currently sidelined with an ACL tear sustained September 2025. The profile is worth understanding now.

description: Plays left wing and cuts inside onto his right foot, with a first instinct that is always goal rather than assist. The goals come not from technical dominance or physical power but from positioning — arriving in the right place at the right time, consistently, in ways that look obvious only after the fact.

Rides the offside line with precise timing, reads deflections before they happen, and positions for second balls while others are still tracking the first. The technical execution is imperfect; the spatial intelligence underneath it is not.

onBall.title: Cuts inside to finish, not to combine.
onBall.text: Receives from the left and the first read is always the shooting lane. The instinct is automatic — it makes him predictable to organized defenses and effective against disorganized ones.

onBall.title: Composed in 1v1 finishing situations.
onBall.text: Calm close to goal even when the setup was not. Low, precise, toward the far corner. The execution holds even when body balance is imperfect. Cold at 17 in senior football.

onBall.title: Beats defenders through timing, not burst.
onBall.text: Reads the defender's weight and goes when they are committed. Around-average separation; relies on reading the moment rather than creating it athletically.

onBall.title: Avoids the left foot in high-leverage moments.
onBall.text: Will take a harder right-foot attempt from a worse angle rather than use the left in a simpler position. The preference is absolute enough to be a habit, not a gap.

offBall.title: Elite blindside timing.
offBall.text: Works the line between offside and onside with precision. The two goals against TSC in the Conference League came from reading the defensive line's movement and committing at the exact moment it was wrong-footed.

offBall.title: Rebound and deflection reader.
offBall.text: Positions for the second ball while others are still tracking the first. The header scored at 178cm in a packed box against bigger defenders was positioning, not athleticism.

offBall.title: Anticipates opposition distribution to press.
offBall.text: Gets in the right place before the ball arrives, which is why the fouls-drawn number is so high — defenders run out of options.

offBall.title: Most dangerous when the game is stretched.
offBall.text: Less effective in slow positional attacks against set defenses. The movement that generates his advantage requires space that compact blocks do not provide.

pure.name: Blindside run timing
pure.desc: Not league-inflated. Present against both Serbian SuperLiga and Conference League opposition. Survives tactical shifts.

pure.name: Box arrival instinct
pure.desc: Finds rebounds and second balls independent of team dominance. The header against bigger defenders at 178cm was positioning, not athleticism.

pure.name: 1v1 finishing composure
pure.desc: Calm in close-range situations under pressure. Holds even when the setup is imperfect. Already stable at 17.

context.name: Pressing effectiveness
context.desc: Anticipates distribution to press intelligently at this level. Whether the sharpness of the press translates against teams with faster recycling needs to be observed at the next level.

system.name: Movement-based value
system.desc: Most dangerous in open, stretched games with high defensive lines. In settled positional attacks against compact blocks the output drops significantly. System matching is not optional.

exposed.name: First-step acceleration post-ACL
exposed.desc: Was below average before the injury. The movement reads that generate goal output depend on timing that a half-step of lost sharpness will compromise. This is the primary unknown in the entire profile.

exposed.name: Creation output
exposed.desc: 5th-percentile shot assists. Usable as a pure finisher; limiting as a complete wide attacker who needs to create as well as score.

athletic.label: Top speed
athletic.value: Above average in stride. More dangerous receiving a ball already moving than generating separation from a standing start.

athletic.label: Acceleration
athletic.value: Below average from static starts before injury. The most significant athletic limitation. He compensates by reading when to move rather than creating the moment athletically.

athletic.label: Endurance
athletic.value: Strong engine. Played 90 minutes in every appearance before the injury. Work rate does not fluctuate with the scoreline.

athletic.label: Injury
athletic.value: Left leg ACL, September 2025. Non-contact tear while running in behind. The key question post-surgery is not whether the speed returns but whether the sharpness of the first step does. Those are different things.

cognitive.label: Spatial awareness
cognitive.value: The defining attribute. Processes where space will be before it exists. The blindside timing and rebound reading are both expressions of the same underlying quality.

cognitive.label: Decision speed
cognitive.value: Fast in transition, inconsistent in compact buildup. Scoring decisions are quick and mostly correct. Playmaking decisions are slower — he sometimes commits to the shot before checking what else is available.

cognitive.label: Risk profile
cognitive.value: Scoring-oriented. Will take the shot when the pass is the better option. The vision is there when he looks — the habit of looking first is what is still forming.

cognitive.label: Adaptability
cognitive.value: More effective in dynamic matches. The spatial reads that generate danger in open play arrive too slowly in compact phases where the windows close faster.

psychological.label: Composure
psychological.value: Exceptional for the context. Calm in 1v1 finishing situations at 17 in senior professional football. Stable after missed chances. The coldness in scoring moments is consistent and visible.

psychological.label: Competitiveness
psychological.value: Engages in every duel regardless of match state, presses at both ends, does not disengage when the game is difficult.

psychological.label: Mental bias
psychological.value: Goal-driven mentality overrides the pass option more often than it should. A minor concern now; more significant as the level rises and creation is expected alongside the finishing.

psychological.label: Rehabilitation
psychological.value: No data on his response to the injury yet. The psychological recovery matters as much as the physical one. Worth watching — how he handles the first setbacks in rehab will say more than the injury itself.

devPriorities.title: Recover first-step sharpness post-surgery.
devPriorities.text: The acceleration was below average before the injury. Everything in this development list sits behind this question in importance — the profile does not unlock at the next level if the physical sharpness to execute the movement reads does not return. That takes 12 to 18 months of match data to answer honestly.

devPriorities.title: Integrate the left foot as a genuine threat.
devPriorities.text: The avoidance is a preference, not a capability gap, which makes it coachable. Higher-level defenses will steer him right consistently. Rehabilitation is the right window to build the habit — technical work is available when physical load is not.

devPriorities.title: Develop a pre-shot scan habit.
devPriorities.text: The 5th-percentile shot assists are not a ceiling — they are a cognitive habit. The same spatial intelligence that generates his goal threat would generate assists if directed outward before the shot decision. It requires a consistent check, not new skills.

devPriorities.title: Build physical robustness in static duels.
devPriorities.text: When defenders get goal-side and hold without space to accelerate through, his effectiveness drops sharply. Upper-body strength and shielding technique are the practical levers. The frame is there; the application is not yet.

prose: The pre-injury score sits closer to 7.5. The blindside timing, the finishing composure, the spatial intelligence — none of those are products of the SuperLiga environment, and none disappear with a knee injury. A club buying the profile as it existed through summer 2025 was buying something genuine.

The 6.0 reflects the honest discount for what is currently unknown. A ligament injury on a winger whose game depends on timing and directional change carries a different risk than the same injury on a central midfielder. The movement patterns that produce the goal output are exactly the ones under the most uncertainty in recovery.

The right acquisition model is patient. Monitor the return, acquire somewhere in the 12 months post-comeback, and commit to the full development cycle. A club that moves before the return data exists is taking a genuine gamble, however attractive the pre-injury profile was.

leagueScores.note: Tempo and physicality expose the first-step deficit before the movement intelligence can establish itself. A viable long-term destination; not a realistic first move.
Spatial awareness and blindside timing fit La Liga's positional structure, where intelligent movement is rewarded. A strong long-term fit once the technical execution becomes more reliable.
Verticality and transition volume suit the off-ball running and pressing instinct. The best top-five environment if the physical recovery is full.
Structured movement fits disciplined systems. Tight marking amplifies the static duel weakness. Can function in a well-defined role; unlikely to dominate.
Physical duel frequency highlights the acceleration limitation. Open phases help, but overall compatibility is lower for a player who depends on timing over burst.
Higher lines and more forgiving tempo allow timing and box instincts to operate without constant physical pressure. The cleanest immediate fit post-return.
Physically demanding enough to test the frame without overwhelming a recovering player. Transition-first setups generate exactly the open-phase conditions where the movement reads are most dangerous.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: Significant injury at 17 on the plant leg of a movement-dependent winger. The cognitive and technical base is strong. Whether the physical base recovers fully enough to unlock them at the next level requires 12 to 18 months of post-return data to answer.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Composure in scoring situations, consistent defensive engagement, stable mentality after errors. The injury response is unobserved; the pre-injury profile gives reason for cautious optimism.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €1M is a reasonable valuation in normal circumstances. Post-surgery the market will discount further — which is exactly when attentive clubs can move on a profile the price does not yet reflect.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: Clearly has a best environment — transition-heavy, high defensive lines, game state that creates stretched phases. In settled positional systems against compact blocks, the output drops significantly.

players.trajectoryNote: A 7 projects as a consistent starter at top-division level in a transition-based system. The ceiling of the profile is higher than the rating suggests. The surgery is the reason it is not higher — if the physical sharpness returns fully, the number moves.

verdict.statement: A movement-driven inside forward whose value is built on timing, spatial intelligence, and finishing composure that does not follow naturally from his technical level. At 17, in senior football, he was already generating and converting chances through cognitive qualities most wingers spend years trying to develop. The profile is worth understanding now, monitoring through recovery, and reassessing when return data gives a clearer picture. The worst-case surgical outcome does not erase the intelligence. It only limits the speed at which it can operate.
travels: Blindside run timing — not league-inflated, survives tactical shifts, present in both transition and settled phases
Box arrival instinct — finds rebounds and second balls independent of team dominance
1v1 finishing composure — calm in close-range situations under pressure, holds even when the setup is imperfect
Anticipation of second actions — reads deflections and loose phases early, impactful independent of structure
Pressing instinct — reads opposition distribution, positions to intercept rather than chase
targeted: First-step recovery post-surgery — below-average before the injury; the primary unknown in the whole profile
Right-foot finishing bias — predictable at higher levels; left-foot development achievable but not yet started
Static duel resistance — loses balance and possession when tightly marked without momentum
Pre-shot scanning habit — takes the shot when the pass is the better option; the vision is there when he looks
Creation output — 5th-percentile shot assists; usable as a pure finisher, limiting as a complete wide attacker
verdict.potentialProjection: A 7 projects as a consistent starter at top-division level in a transition-based system. The ceiling of the profile is higher than the rating suggests. The surgery is the reason it is not higher — if the physical sharpness returns fully, the number moves. If it does not, it does not.

players.verdictLine: A 17-year-old on loan at Grafičar Beograd from Red Star who ranks among the top forwards in the Serbian SuperLiga on both creation and defensive contribution simultaneously. The same player. The same position. At the same time.

description: Plays right wing on his left foot, cutting inside to threat with the stronger foot rather than crossing from the touchline. First-step acceleration is the primary physical weapon — he creates separation quickly and attacks space before defenders can set.

The half-space between fullback and center-back is his natural habitat, used both for shooting lanes and as the channel for vertical runs in behind. At this stage his game is driven by individual action over combination, but the creation numbers suggest the capacity to do both is already present.

onBall.title: Attacks his marker directly.
onBall.text: Actively isolates and drives at defenders, especially in wide areas. Comfortable engaging the same defender repeatedly rather than recycling possession.

onBall.title: Keeps the ball tight under contact.
onBall.text: Very tight control while dribbling, rarely letting it travel away from his feet. Prioritises balance and control over pushing the ball into space — which explains the paradox of elite dribble success alongside low acceleration-with-ball numbers.

onBall.title: Cuts inside to shoot.
onBall.text: Strong preference for receiving on the left and driving inward onto the stronger foot. Looks for the shooting lane early rather than drifting wide to cross.

onBall.title: Shoots frequently and with conviction.
onBall.text: Will pull the trigger from a variety of distances and angles without hesitation. Favours far-post finishes. Does not need a perfect setup.

onBall.title: Decision outcome varies in identical situations.
onBall.text: Can choose to dribble, shoot, or pass without a consistent hierarchy. The playmaking option exists — 99th-percentile shot assists confirm the vision — but it is not always selected.

offBall.title: Explodes forward in transition.
offBall.text: Immediate forward runs on ball recovery, one of the first players to sprint into attacking lanes. Looks to attack space aggressively rather than waiting for the play to develop.

offBall.title: Consistent blindside and vertical runs.
offBall.text: Targets the FB–CB gap with timing that shows up across multiple match contexts. Not role-protected — the movement survives in different tactical setups.

offBall.title: Tracks back with genuine intensity.
offBall.text: Recovers shape quickly, shows real effort in defensive phases. 92nd-percentile defensive actions for a winger signals willingness, not just proximity.

offBall.title: Presses to win the ball, not just discourage.
offBall.text: Actively closes down, engages defensively rather than jogging through press phases. The 96th-percentile pAdj tackles and interceptions is not a positional accident.

pure.name: First-step acceleration
pure.desc: Explosive over 3–5 metres. Straight-line separation is a defining weapon independent of system or opposition quality.

pure.name: Elite creation output
pure.desc: 99th-percentile shot assists and 97th-percentile xA at 17. The spatial reads required to set up shots for teammates do not inflate with soft opposition.

pure.name: Defensive pressing instinct
pure.desc: 96th-percentile pAdj Tkl+Int for a winger. Active pressing that produces results, not positional proximity. The most rare combination at this age.

context.name: Contact dribbling
context.desc: 91st-percentile success built on balance and direction change against defenders who commit early. Works reliably at this level; the question is how it holds when defenders wait rather than commit.

system.name: Game-state dependence
system.desc: Involvement fluctuates in slower match phases. Best in transition-heavy, open games. In controlled positional systems, engagement and impact both drop.

exposed.name: Finishing efficiency
exposed.desc: 54th-percentile goals against 83rd-percentile shot quality positions. The conversion is not yet matching the setup — coachable, but the gap is real.

exposed.name: Physical duel resistance
exposed.desc: Upper-body still developing. Can be displaced in sustained shoulder-to-shoulder contact at this level; more so at the next.

athletic.label: Acceleration
athletic.value: Explosive over first 3–5 metres. Straight-line separation is a defining weapon. The 7th-percentile accelerations-with-ball figure describes a different phenomenon — tight ball-carrying deliberately slows him, not an athletic limitation.

athletic.label: Agility
athletic.value: Sharp directional changes, balance at speed. The ability to execute sharp cuts while keeping the ball close is the technical expression of the athletic base.

athletic.label: Physical maturity
athletic.value: Upper-body duel strength still developing. Can be displaced in sustained shoulder-to-shoulder contact. The 1.81m frame provides the base; the application in physical duels is not yet consistent.

athletic.label: Recovery speed
athletic.value: Good in open phases. Less effective under repeated physical engagement — the defensive effort is genuine but the physical cost accumulates across long sequences.

cognitive.label: Decision speed
cognitive.value: Aggressive but inconsistent under pressure. In-transition decisions are fast and mostly correct. In compact buildup, the tendency to hold the ball too long is the visible cognitive lag.

cognitive.label: Spatial awareness
cognitive.value: Strong in transition. Half-space reads and vertical run timing are already consistent. Positioning in settled phases still developing — less reliable when the game is slow.

cognitive.label: Risk profile
cognitive.value: Action-first over control. Takes responsibility, seeks involvement, not afraid of difficult situations. The tendency to choose complex options over simple ones is the same instinct that produces elite creation numbers.

cognitive.label: Adaptability
cognitive.value: Better in open games than structured systems. Involvement fluctuates in slower match phases — the cognitive engagement tracks the physical freedom available.

psychological.label: Bravery
psychological.value: Demands the ball and embraces 1v1s. Not afraid to take responsibility in attacking phases. The 96th-percentile fouls-drawn reflects a player who invites contact rather than avoiding it.

psychological.label: Involvement
psychological.value: Can drift when uninvolved. Engagement level in slower phases is lower than in transition moments. A coaching target that becomes more important as the level of opposition rises.

psychological.label: Defensive attitude
psychological.value: The defensive numbers are not produced by a player who was told to press. They suggest a genuine instinct — the concentration consistency must improve, but the foundation is real.

psychological.label: Composure
psychological.value: Finishing efficiency below chance volume suggests the composure in the box is not yet matching the composure in the duel. The shot selection quality (83rd-percentile npxG per shot) is there; the end-product consistency is not.

devPriorities.title: Build a decision hierarchy in the final third.
devPriorities.text: The 99th-percentile shot assists and 97th-percentile xA confirm the creative ceiling is real. The goal numbers (54th percentile) confirm it is not yet being realised. The same player who sets up the chance needs a clearer internal answer to when he takes it versus gives it. That is a cognitive task, not a technical one.

devPriorities.title: Composure and shot selection inside the box.
devPriorities.text: The npxG per shot at the 83rd percentile tells you the positions are good. The conversion rate at the 55th percentile tells you the execution is not yet matching them. Finishing under pressure — particularly when the ball-body shape is not ideal — is the specific gap to close.

devPriorities.title: Upper-body strength for duel resistance.
devPriorities.text: Can be displaced in sustained shoulder-to-shoulder contact. At higher levels, defenders get physical before the separation is created. Building upper-body strength and improving ball-shielding technique protects the dribbling advantage that currently defines him.

devPriorities.title: Consistent involvement in slower match phases.
devPriorities.text: The fluctuation in engagement when the game is controlled rather than chaotic is a concentration habit, not a motivational problem. Learning to generate danger in settled possession — rather than waiting for the game to open — is what separates a transition-only threat from a complete wide attacker.

prose: The acceleration, the dribbling quality, the creation output, and the defensive contribution are not products of the environment. A player with 99th-percentile shot assists at 17 in this league is doing something that does not inflate with soft opposition — the spatial reads required to set up shots for teammates travel unconditionally.

The 2.5-point discount is honest. Finishing consistency, decision volatility in the final third, and physical duel resistance are all genuine limitations at the next level. None of them destroy the profile. All of them are coachable. The systemic risk is real — this profile requires space and transition to operate at its ceiling, and not every environment provides that.

The right immediate environment is transition-heavy, high defensive lines, and gives wide players freedom to engage rather than hold position. Eredivisie or Jupiler Pro League as a first step. A system that asks him to press, carry, and create in rapid sequences will get the best version immediately.

leagueScores.note: First-step burst is real but duels are stronger and space closes faster — decision volatility and finishing inconsistency get exposed before the other qualities can establish themselves. Viable long-term with physical development; not the right first move.

Off-ball timing and half-space comfort fit La Liga's positional structure. Compact blocks and controlled buildup slow attacks and reduce separation efficiency. Can function with refinement; the style doesn't fully suit the current profile.

The most natural top-five fit. Verticality, open transitions, and willingness to attack space align with his acceleration and direct style. Consistency issues remain in tighter moments, but the league's pace and chaos protect his strengths more than others.

Movement and willingness to run behind defensive lines translate. Tight marking and compact structures punish decision volatility. Can operate here, but development would require tactical maturity that is still forming.

Athletic profile helps in isolated duels and broken phases. Physical contact intensity and wide defenders who engage physically test the current strength base. Open phases suit him; controlled physical battles do not.
High lines, forgiving tempo, and frequent attacking sequences allow timing and acceleration to operate without constant physical stress. The cleanest developmental bridge for this profile.

Physical demand is real enough to build duel resistance without overwhelming a still-developing body. Transition-first clubs generate the space his game requires. A strong alternative to the Eredivisie.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: No injury history. Strong upward trajectory at 17. Starting for a senior loan club, producing elite numbers in creation and defensive contribution simultaneously. The finishing and decision consistency are genuine questions, but both are coachable with the right environment.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
