The ceiling is higher — a player this complete at 22, being suppressed by his environment, has room to grow into a 9-level contributor given the right setting and coaching. The temperament is the only variable that could prevent the move.

players.verdictLine: A 21-year-old who leads the 1. HNL in box presence and aerial duels among all midfielders — not because he controls games, but because he crashes them. The profile is specific, unusual, and considerably more transferable than it first appears.

description: Covers the full vertical range of the pitch with and without the ball. The attacking orientation is dominant: he positions high, attacks second balls, and arrives into the box with the timing and aggression of a striker rather than a midfielder.
Best deployed as the more advanced of two central midfielders, in a 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3, where he can operate between the lines and arrive late into the box without leaving a structural gap.

onBall.title: Box arrival timing
onBall.text: His most distinctive offensive habit: he reads crosses and set pieces early and attacks the ball in the air while defenders are still reacting. The majority of his goals this season have been headers, which is unusual for a midfielder and reflects genuine spatial anticipation, not athleticism alone.

onBall.title: Ball carrying over distance
onBall.text: He covers ground with the ball, particularly in transition. His progressive run percentile (98th among HNL midfielders) reflects a habit of driving rather than releasing. He keeps the ball close and changes direction under pressure rather than passing out of it — a Barcelona academy residue that makes him hard to press in isolation.

onBall.title: Two-footed execution
onBall.text: He is right-footed but regularly uses his left for passes and finishes in traffic. The frequency is notable — this is not a desperation foot, it is a functional second option that he deploys selectively.

onBall.title: Short passing accuracy with tempo lapses
onBall.text: His short passing accuracy is around the 16th percentile in the midfield group, but the mechanism matters. When he plays short, he is accurate. The problem is that he sometimes forces tempo and tries to get rid of the ball quickly — the passes that go wrong are the hurried ones rather than the deliberate ones.

onBall.title: First touch inconsistency
onBall.text: When the ball is played sharply into a tight area, his first touch occasionally bounces too far or leaves him side-on. This is not constant — his close control in dribbling situations is genuinely good — but the receive-under-pressure touch is the part of his technical profile that still needs cleaning up.

offBall.title: Roaming movement pattern
offBall.text: He does not stay in one channel. He scans quickly and regularly appears in wide areas, then returns centrally, which confuses man-marking systems and creates positional overloads in the final third. The habit is an asset when the team has the ball; it creates exposure in transition when he is out wide and the team loses possession.

offBall.title: Set-piece positioning
offBall.text: He positions himself specifically for deliveries — near post, back post, the penalty spot — and times his run to arrive late. This is a deliberate movement pattern, not coincidental, and it is what makes his aerial win rate at 100th percentile among HNL midfielders credible despite his frame not being unusually large.

offBall.title: Pressing effort without structure
offBall.text: He presses with energy when the team presses but does not lead the press. His defensive actions against the forward group sit in the 75th percentile, which is real output, but his positioning in recovery is inconsistent. He drops back often but rarely deep.

offBall.title: Transition jogging
offBall.text: A noted tendency to jog rather than sprint in transition, particularly back toward his own half. At Hajduk, who control possession and lead games frequently, this is partially contextual. At a higher level, this will be tested directly and should be monitored.

offBall.title: Tackling quality
offBall.text: When he commits to a tackle, his timing is generally good. His sliding and standing tackle success is solid, and his anticipatory positioning in ground duels — using his frame to shield and redirect — compensates for a strength level that is below average for his size.

pure.name: Box Arrival Intelligence
pure.desc: His ability to read the moment a cross or delivery is played and arrive at the right spot is not pace-dependent. It is a habit of attention — he reads the first phase and positions for the second before most defenders have processed the first. This survives any system or league environment.

pure.name: Two-Footed Execution
pure.desc: Functional use of the left foot in real game situations — not just a fallback. This is set by this age and reflects his Barcelona academy background. Translates directly across environments: defenders cannot shade one side and feel protected.

pure.name: Aerial Heading Ability
pure.desc: He generates power and accuracy from a standing or running jump that outperforms his physical frame. The technical habit — attacking the ball first rather than jumping and hoping — is what produces this. Reliable and transferable.

pure.name: Athletic Genetics
pure.desc: Son of a Lithuanian marathon runner and triple jumper. His natural physical attributes — vertical jump, body composition, recovery capacity — reflect that background. The frame is mesomorphic without being bulky, which should serve him well as he fills out physically.

context.name: Dribbling Effectiveness
context.desc: 89th percentile dribble success against HNL midfielders, 93rd against forwards. Strong in isolation, against a single opponent in space. In tighter, more intense defensive environments this number will come under pressure.

context.name: Shot Creation for Others
context.desc: His xA (76th vs midfielders, 64th vs forwards) suggests he generates half-chances for teammates when he carries into dangerous areas. But this is incidental to his primary mechanism. A system that asks him to be a primary chance creator will find his output drops.

system.name: High Box Presence
system.desc: At Hajduk, who dominate possession and generate cross volume, he arrives in the box frequently. The number — 100th percentile touches in box among HNL midfielders — reflects a system that creates the conditions for him to exploit. The quality remains even as volume compresses.

system.name: Progressive Running Volume
system.desc: 98th percentile progressive runs. In a Hajduk side that transitions quickly and has space to exploit, he drives forward constantly. In a lower block or a team that builds slowly, this trait is compressed. The capacity is there; the environment either activates it or suppresses it.

exposed.name: Pass Selection Under Pressure
exposed.desc: His passing percentile vs midfielders (9th passes per 90, 30th accuracy) is low. The problem is not volume but the rushed passes when he is pressed: he forces it rather than retaining. This is a habit question, not a technical question, which makes it addressable but not automatic.

exposed.name: Defensive Transition Intensity
exposed.desc: The jogging tendency in recovery is partly contextual, partly habitual. At the next level, where pressing intensity is maintained for longer periods, the habit will be exposed in specific match situations.

exposed.name: Injury Resilience (Monitor)
exposed.desc: A cruciate ligament strain in 2023 (70 days, 11 games missed) and an ankle injury in late 2025 (54 days, 4 games missed). Two significant soft-tissue injuries before the age of 22 warrant monitoring.

athletic.label: Frame
athletic.value: 1.81m, mesomorphic build. Slight frame relative to height — not bulky, but not slight. Core strength is sufficient for ground duels currently; physical growth over the next two to three years should close the gap at a higher level.

athletic.label: Pace & Acceleration
athletic.value: Decent, not elite. Covers ground well over distance, turns quickly in tight spaces. His 72nd percentile accelerations are functional rather than explosive.

athletic.label: Aerial Ability
athletic.value: 100th percentile aerial duels per 90 against both midfielders and forwards. Exceptional vertical leap, strong heading technique. The output is not size-dependent — it is timing and aggression.

athletic.label: Strength
athletic.value: Below average for his frame, above average in application. Core positioning in duels compensates for raw strength. Not yet a dominant physical presence in contact.

athletic.label: Injury Context
athletic.value: Two significant injuries before age 22. Cruciate strain (2023), ankle (2025–26). Both recovered within expected timelines. Screening recommended at this valuation.

cognitive.label: Spatial Awareness
cognitive.value: High, particularly in the final third. His box arrival patterns reflect genuine anticipatory reading of the game's next phase. He processes where the ball will land before it leaves the delivery.

cognitive.label: Scanning
cognitive.value: Quick scanner in both phases. He checks his surroundings before receiving, which partly explains his ability to find pocket space. The scanning habit is clearly established — a Barcelona academy marker.

cognitive.label: Decision Speed Under Pressure
cognitive.value: Inconsistent. His best decisions are instinctive — the carry, the box run, the header. When pressed into a passing decision quickly, the tempo occasionally gets ahead of his processing and forces a rushed ball.

cognitive.label: Positional Flexibility
cognitive.value: High tactical adaptability. Can operate as an AM, central 8, or wider midfielder. His versatility is genuine because his scanning and movement patterns do not depend on a fixed starting position.

psychological.label: Competitive Background
psychological.value: Born into elite athletic family. Both parents competed at international level in endurance and power disciplines. The psychological baseline for competition and physical dedication appears embedded rather than coached.

psychological.label: Barcelona Academy Exit
psychological.value: Left Barça, joined Hajduk at 17. Moving from one of the world's most demanding development environments to a first-tier Balkan club required genuine adaptability. The transition has produced consistent senior minutes and tangible output.

psychological.label: Composure in the Box
psychological.value: Strong. His npxG per shot at the 96th percentile among HNL midfielders, 89th among forwards, suggests he is generating and converting high-quality opportunities rather than wasting them.

psychological.label: Communication Style
psychological.value: Quiet on the pitch. Not a vocal organizer. He leads through movement and effort rather than instruction. This is not a concern at 21 — it is a personality type, not a maturity gap.

devPriorities.title: Pass selection in high-pressure moments
devPriorities.text: The technical range is there — he executes short passes accurately and can use both feet. What he does not yet do consistently is slow down to make the right decision when the press arrives. He forces tempo on the receive and the result is occasional turnovers from rushed balls. This is a processing habit, not a technical limitation.

devPriorities.title: Defensive transition intensity
devPriorities.text: The jogging pattern in recovery is observable and documented. At a higher level, the physical cost of consistent sprint-back defending will test this habit directly. He does not currently treat defensive transition as urgently as he treats attacking transition. That needs to change at the next level.

devPriorities.title: First touch quality on sharp, low deliveries
devPriorities.text: His aerial receive is excellent. His ground receive in tight situations still produces errors — the ball occasionally bounces away from his body or leaves him side-on when he needs to be facing forward. The inconsistency in this specific context is visible enough to name.

devPriorities.title: Physical assertiveness in contact
devPriorities.text: His strength application is not yet matching his physical foundation. He wins aerial duels well because his jumping technique is excellent. In ground contact situations, he is functional rather than dominant. This should develop naturally as he fills out physically over the next two to three seasons.

prose: The mechanism of Pukštas's value is unusually portable. His primary mechanism — arriving into dangerous zones, attacking aerial deliveries, and finishing from close range — is not system-dependent in the same way most midfielders' output is. Boxes exist everywhere. Set pieces happen everywhere.

What the 1. HNL inflates is his volume. Hajduk dominate possession and generate cross volume that creates the conditions for his second-phase arrivals to happen frequently. At a higher level that volume will reduce, but the quality-per-opportunity holds: his npxG per shot at 89th percentile against the forward pool is a finishing habit, not a system artifact.

He is ready for most target leagues now, particularly those with verticality and cross-volume in their game model. The risks worth naming are physical intensity and pressing demands — the recovery jogging habit will be tested in leagues where box-to-box midfielders are expected to cover ground at sprint pace in defensive transitions.

leagueScores.note: The box-arrival mechanism translates directly. Best fit: a direct, vertical club rather than a possession-dominant one that asks the eight to build play. Not an automatic starter, but a credible squad option with room to grow.

The strongest single fit. High-intensity pressing environments demand athleticism and vertical drive — both present in this profile. Cross volume and set-piece delivery would give him frequent arrivals. Primary target league.

Good fit, slightly less obvious than Bundesliga. Ligue 1 rewards athletic, box-to-box profiles with goal threat, and his defensive output — functional rather than elite — is acceptable in a league where eights are not always required to be defensive anchors.

More demanding positionally. Serie A midfielders are asked to do more structured defensive work and operate in tighter spaces. An attacking-midfield or trequartista position in a direct Italian side is a better fit than a central-eight role.

La Liga's best clubs ask midfielders to circulate possession and manage tempo — neither is his strength. The lower-half of La Liga, with more direct, counter-attacking teams, is a better fit. System match at elite La Liga level is the weakest of the target leagues.

Near-ideal system fit as a developmental step. High cross volume, attacking-oriented football, physical but not extreme pressing demands. Would give him box arrivals, set-piece opportunities, and competitive minutes.

Comfortable transition. Belgian football is physically direct, rewards athletes, and has the set-piece and cross volume to activate his strongest traits. A Club Brugge or Anderlecht system profile would be very well matched.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: 2,278 senior minutes at 21, consistent output across the full season, already in the top-10 U21 shooting midfielders globally per CIES data. The injury history (two events before age 22) introduces a small flag. The trajectory is clearly upward.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Elite athletic family background, Barcelona academy formation, successful transition to a different culture at 17. He has already managed the psychological complexity of two significant career transitions and come out with increasing output.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €5M at a club with a 2027 contract. CAA Stellar is a professional and established agency. The CIES ranking, Hajduk's profile, and the US eligibility all create market pressure. The value is fair for this output level.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: The core mechanism — box arrivals, aerial threat, progressive carrying — is not system-dependent in the way a pure playmaker's output is. The system risk is real only in possession-dominant, tempo-control environments that ask the eight to be a primary ball-progressor.

players.trajectoryNote: Projects as a consistent contributor at a top-five European club — a player who regularly delivers eight to twelve league goals from midfield, provides aerial threat at set pieces, and drives transitions at pace.

verdict.statement: Pukštas is not a midfielder who scores. He is a scorer who plays in midfield — a distinction that matters both for how you evaluate him and how you deploy him. His box-arrival intelligence, aerial finishing quality, and two-footed execution are formed habits, not projections. The passing volume is low, the defensive positioning is functional rather than excellent, and he occasionally forces tempo rather than managing it. A club that buys him understanding the profile — a direct, goal-contributing eight in a vertical system — gets a player whose primary mechanism has already proved itself against a decent level of competition.
travels: Box arrival timing and second-phase positioning — a habit of attention, not a system outcome
Shot quality selection — 89th percentile npxG per shot against the forward pool; the efficiency is genuine
Aerial heading — 100th percentile against both comparison groups, driven by timing and technique, not frame size
Two-footed execution — a genuine second option from Barcelona academy formation, not a fallback
Dribble success and close control under pressure — 93rd percentile against forwards in isolation situations
Psychological resilience — elite athletic background, two significant injury recoveries, consistent output trajectory
targeted: Rushed passing under pressure — forcing tempo when pressed is the first habit top opponents will exploit
Defensive transition intensity — the recovery jogging pattern will be tested in every environment above this one
First touch on sharp ground deliveries — inconsistent enough at this level to become a genuine problem at the next
Physical assertiveness in contact — raw force in collisions does not yet match frame and genetic baseline
Injury monitoring — two soft-tissue events before age 22; full medical screening required at €5M
verdict.potentialProjection: An 8 projects as a consistent contributor at a top-five European club — a player who regularly delivers eight to twelve league goals from midfield, provides aerial threat at set pieces that opponents do not expect from a midfielder, and drives transitions at pace. The ceiling depends less on further development than on system fit.

players.verdictLine: Plays with the composure and tactical intelligence of someone three or four years older. The playmaking output is genuine and already elite for his league.

A 19-year-old inside forward who ranked 2nd among all U21 wingers in Europe's non-big-five leagues on the CIES/Impect index. The explosiveness is genuine, the creation numbers are strong, and the left-foot dependency is the thing to watch.

description: Receives wide on the right and drives inward onto his left foot, looking for the shot lane rather than the crossing position. His value is in the first two seconds after receiving: the first step separates him from his marker before defenders can set, and what follows is pressure, chaos, and usually a left-footed attempt from wherever he ends up.

Best behind a striker with width provided by the fullback. The 7th-ranked player in accelerations per 90 among all U21 offensive players in top divisions by SkillCorner tells you the engine is real before you see a single touch.

onBall.title: Attacks his marker immediately upon receiving.
onBall.text: Does not check the ball or reset. The decision to drive at the defender is made before contact, which means the first step is already in motion by the time the opponent reacts. This is what the acceleration numbers measure; they describe a choice, not just a physical attribute.

onBall.title: Keeps the ball tight under contact from multiple defenders.
onBall.text: Body posture looks precarious while dribbling — a slight forward lean that makes him look like he might lose the ball — but he almost never does. The balance is functional, not aesthetic. Balkan defenders who want to kill the play find him surprisingly difficult to dispossess.

onBall.title: Cuts inside onto his left and shoots with conviction from any angle.
onBall.text: The 73rd-percentile shots volume and shoot-first mentality mean defenders cannot settle into passive positions. He will pull the trigger from positions where most wingers would recycle. Whether it goes in is secondary to whether the habit forces defensive reactions.

onBall.title: Creates for teammates off the dribble with genuine quality.
onBall.text: 87th-percentile shot assists and 73rd-percentile xA identify him as a winger who creates for others while also pursuing his own shot. Smart passes at the 81st percentile suggest the vision is there even when he chooses not to use it.

onBall.title: Overwhelmingly left-footed in execution.
onBall.text: Almost every shot, every significant dribble, every cross attempt involves the left foot. The right foot exists for shielding and balance. At higher levels, where defenders have more time to set and force the weaker foot, it will be targeted.

offBall.title: Explodes forward immediately on ball recovery.
offBall.text: One of the first players to attack space in transition. Does not wait to confirm possession is secure — reads the moment the press is won and commits immediately to the attacking run.

offBall.title: Positions wide and early to create 1v1 opportunities.
offBall.text: Drops to receive away from defenders in wide channels, looking to isolate rather than combine. The move is about manufacturing the duel, not about linking play.

offBall.title: Tracks back and presses with genuine intensity.
offBall.text: 76th-percentile successful defensive actions and 64th-percentile pAdj tackles and interceptions are meaningful for an inside forward. The defensive contribution reflects willingness and tempo, not repositioning reluctantly. A right midfielder role is viable if the occasion demands it.

offBall.title: Uses momentum rather than physical wrestling.
offBall.text: Avoids prolonged contact situations and does not try to hold ground against defenders who outweigh him. Looks to attack space before defenders can set their feet. The 1.80m frame is adequate; the light-contact preference is a deliberate choice rather than a physical limitation.

pure.name: First-step acceleration
pure.desc: 7th among all U21 offensive players in top divisions by accelerations per 90. The separation is immediate — the threat is created before defenders can respond.

pure.name: Ball retention under contact
pure.desc: The unconventional forward lean conceals genuine body control. Balkan senior defenders find him difficult to dispossess. This travels.

pure.name: Creation output
pure.desc: 87th-percentile shot assists, 88th-percentile cross accuracy, 81st-percentile smart passes. The vision and delivery exist alongside the shoot-first profile.

context.name: Pressing intensity
context.desc: 76th-percentile defensive actions is real, but the opposition quality in the SuperLiga is a factor. The willingness is genuine; the difficulty level will increase.

system.name: Defensive resilience without the ball
system.desc: Best in transition-heavy systems that create wide isolations. In settled possession structures his influence drops and the decision inconsistency is exposed in tighter spaces.

exposed.name: Right foot
exposed.desc: Almost every significant action uses the left foot. Defenders at higher levels will steer him right and wait. The development window is now.

exposed.name: Shot quality
exposed.desc: 13th-percentile npxG per shot. Volume compensates currently; at the next level, where closing speed is faster, it will not fully compensate.

athletic.label: Acceleration
athletic.value: The headline attribute. 7th among all U21 offensive players in top divisions by accelerations per 90 per SkillCorner. The separation in the first two steps is what makes him difficult to plan against.

athletic.label: Endurance
athletic.value: Strong engine. Maintains pressing intensity and attacking runs across 90 minutes. Nearly 2,000 minutes at Radnik without an injury history suggests the physical base is robust.

athletic.label: Agility
athletic.value: Sharp directional changes with good balance. The odd dribbling posture conceals genuine body control — the lean that looks like he might fall is the same lean that keeps defenders guessing.

athletic.label: Strength
athletic.value: Light frame. Less effective when defenders get tight and hold him off with body weight. At higher levels, more defenders will make that contact deliberately.

cognitive.label: Decision speed
cognitive.value: Fast when instinctive, inconsistent when multiple options are present. When the decision is drive-and-shoot, execution is immediate. When the game asks him to choose between dribble, pass, and shoot simultaneously, the hesitation is visible.

cognitive.label: Risk profile
cognitive.value: Aggressive and consistent. Prefers action over control, takes responsibility, and seeks the ball rather than hiding after errors. High-variance: produces danger repeatedly, but also cedes possession in ways more conservative players would not.

cognitive.label: Spatial awareness
cognitive.value: Good in transition and in wide isolation. Reads 1v1 positions intelligently. Weaker in settled positional phases where movement is less intuitive.

cognitive.label: Decision hierarchy
cognitive.value: Similar situations can produce dribble, shot, or pass without a visible pattern. The vision is there — 81st-percentile smart passes — but the trigger for using it is not yet reliable.

psychological.label: Mentality
psychological.value: Fighter. Demands the ball, embraces duels, presses willingly. No evidence of avoiding involvement after errors. The shoot-first profile is a psychological as much as technical feature.

psychological.label: Consistency
psychological.value: Impact arrives in bursts rather than as sustained control. A 7/30 shooting run does not appear to alter either volume or approach. The resilience to sustain a high-attempt model despite variable returns is a real attribute.

psychological.label: Big game performance
psychological.value: Stable confidence across match contexts. Composure under defensive pressure from multiple defenders simultaneously is a genuine psychological strength.

psychological.label: Durability
psychological.value: No injury history. ~1,928 minutes at Radnik. Physical and psychological availability across a full season, at 19, with this intensity of movement and contact volume, is worth noting.

devPriorities.title: Build a clearer decision hierarchy in the final third.
devPriorities.text: The raw materials for all three outcomes — dribble, shoot, pass — are present. The 87th-percentile shot assists confirm he can create for teammates; the 81st-percentile smart passes confirm the vision exists. What is inconsistent is the selection process when all three options are available. Reducing volatility in identical situations will lift the end-product reliability significantly without requiring any new skills.

devPriorities.title: Develop the right foot as at least a viable threat.
devPriorities.text: The left-foot dependency is currently manageable because he generates directional variety spatially — going both ways while finishing left. At higher levels, where defensive shape is better organized and defenders have more time to steer him right, the absence of a right-foot threat becomes a structural problem. The development window is now, not later.

devPriorities.title: Improve physical robustness in contact situations.
devPriorities.text: The frame at 1.80m is there. The habit of avoiding prolonged contact is sensible now but will limit effectiveness when defenders in better leagues routinely get tight and hold ground. Shielding technique and the ability to draw fouls from physical positioning — rather than from momentum alone — is the next layer of the profile.

prose: The acceleration, the dribbling under pressure, the defensive willingness, and the creation output are not products of the SuperLiga environment. They are observable in how he plays regardless of the opposition quality. Those travel. A club acquiring Owusu knows what it is getting in the first two seconds of any attacking sequence.

The score sits at 7.5 because the left-foot dependency and the decision inconsistency are genuine questions at the next level, not minor concerns. They are coachable — the ranking among U21 wingers across Europe confirms the physical and creative base is real — but coachable is not the same as already resolved. The right system speeds that resolution considerably.

The best immediate environment is one that creates space through vertical play, tolerates high-attempt profiles, and does not ask him to hold width patiently. Eredivisie and Jupiler Pro League are the structural fits. La Liga's technical environment would accelerate the creative development but expose the decision inconsistency in tighter spaces before it is ready to handle them.

leagueScores.note: Explosiveness and directness translate but decision volatility and physical fragility in contact situations would be punished before the other qualities could establish themselves. Not the right first move out of the SuperLiga.

The technical environment suits the dribbling quality. The slower rhythm and compactness of defensive blocks expose decision inconsistency and the left-foot dependency more than a transition-heavy league would.
Transition-heavy play, wide isolations, and vertical attacks amplify the acceleration and 1v1 threat. The best top-five environment for his profile right now, though not the optimal first step.
Tactical structure limits freedom to attack instinctively. Decision hesitation is punished in tight spaces. The profile does not fit Serie A's typical demands for wingers in positional structures.

Athletic pace and isolation opportunities suit the profile. The open nature of Ligue 1 defensive structures gives him room to generate momentum before defenders can get set. Impact likely streaky, but present.

Open games, high transitions, and frequent 1v1 opportunities maximize explosiveness and confidence. The natural first step out of the SuperLiga for this profile — and likely where the decision hierarchy develops fastest.

Physical demands test the frame without overwhelming it. Belgian clubs regularly deploy direct wide players in exactly the transition-first structures that suit him. A legitimate bridge alongside the Eredivisie.

riskGauges.label: Development
riskGauges.note: No injury history, nearly 2,000 senior minutes at 19, ranked 2nd among U21 wingers in Europe's non-big-five leagues. The left-foot dependency and decision consistency are the open questions — neither is a ceiling, both are coachable with the right environment.

riskGauges.label: Psychological
riskGauges.note: Resilient after errors, demands the ball in difficult moments, presses willingly. The shoot-first mentality that continues regardless of recent returns signals a psychological profile that is not fragile.

riskGauges.label: Market
riskGauges.note: €700K for the 2nd-ranked U21 winger in non-big-five Europe is not a valuation that reflects the output. The market has not yet processed what the CIES ranking, the SkillCorner acceleration data, and the top-10 U20 take-on chart are collectively saying.

riskGauges.label: Systemic
riskGauges.note: A clearly defined best environment: vertical, transition-heavy, wide isolations, tolerance for high-attempt profiles. In settled possession systems or compact defensive structures, the impact drops significantly.

players.trajectoryNote: A 7 projects as a regular starter at top-division level in a transition-based league, with consistent attacking threat through pace and 1v1 ability. If the decision hierarchy and right-foot threat develop, the number moves.

verdict.statement: A high-variance, high-threat inside forward whose game is built on explosiveness, 1v1 dominance, and a shoot-first mentality. He consistently creates danger through individual action, running past defenders as though they do not exist and keeping the ball under pressure that would force most wide players to release early. The ceiling is defined by two things: whether the decision hierarchy in the final third stabilises, and whether a right-foot threat ever develops. Both are open questions. The floor, even without those developments, is a winger who contributes at a level that justifies significantly more than the current valuation.
travels: Elite acceleration and 1v1 separation — top-10 U20 take-ons globally, 7th in U21 accelerations per 90 across top divisions
Ball retention under contact from multiple defenders — tight body control despite unconventional posture
Creation output — 87th-percentile shot assists, 88th-percentile cross accuracy, 81st-percentile smart passes
Defensive work rate — 76th-percentile defensive actions, viable in right midfield role
Mental resilience — shoot-first model sustained regardless of recent conversion returns
targeted: Left-foot dependency — almost every shot and significant dribble is left-footed; defenders at higher levels will force this immediately
Shot quality — 13th-percentile npxG per shot; volume compensates now, will not fully compensate at the next level
Decision hierarchy — identical situations can produce dribble, shoot, or pass without a consistent hierarchy
Physical robustness in sustained contact — avoids prolonged duels; defenders who get tight and hold ground reduce effectiveness
verdict.potentialProjection: Regular starter at top-division level in a transition-based league. Consistent attacking threat through pace and 1v1 ability, with a genuine secondary contribution through creation and pressing. A 7 feels conservative given the CIES ranking and the physical data — but it reflects honest uncertainty about whether the left-foot dependency and shot quality gap close sufficiently for the ceiling to move higher. If they do, the number moves.
